_meta.purpose: Curated named-bill -> H.R./S.-number crosswalk for the bill cross-check tool (lda_bill_lookup.py). Fixes cleanup-note C (bill name inconsistency) and the exact gap that killed L004: press releases and members name bills ('the Farm Bill', 'NDAA', 'Inflation Reduction Act'); LDA filings cite H.R./S. numbers. Number-only matching therefore fabricates 'lobbied but publicly silent' bills. This table lets a query by name reach the number-cited filings, and a query by number surface the name-cited press.
discipline: Precision over recall, same rule as build_db.py's ISSUE_KEYWORDS. Every 'phrases' string is a distinctive, whole-word-matchable name, never a bare topic word.

'names' are for display and reverse lookup (number -> which famous bill). 'phrases' are the ONLY strings used for free-text name matching. Ambiguous acronyms (e.g. 'IRA' = also individual retirement account) go in 'names' but NOT in 'phrases'.

'bills' are normalized numbers (build_db.py norm_bill(): prefix letters + digits, e.g. 'HR5376', 'S2938') joined against bill_mentions. bill_mentions has NO Congress dimension, so a low, reused number (H.R.1, H.R.2, S.1) refers to a different bill each Congress and is UNSAFE to list. Such bills are left phrase-primary (empty 'bills') on purpose; lda_bill_lookup.py additionally warns whenever a matched number spans filings from more than one Congress cohort.
Every entry is cited to its congress.gov page. Enacted-law numbers are stable public record; recurring vehicles (NDAA) list one number per fiscal year with its Public Law.

congress_windows.note: Corpus covers filings/press 2022-2026 Q1, i.e. the tail of the 117th through the start of the 119th. A single normalized number can therefore be ambiguous across three Congresses; see the H.R.1 warning behavior in lda_bill_lookup.py.

aliases.note: Enacted as H.R.5376 (117th). 'IRA' is intentionally a display name only, not a match phrase (collides with 'individual retirement account'). H.R.5376 also began life as the Build Back Better vehicle.
Enacted as H.R.4346 (117th). CHIPS = Creating Helpful Incentives to Produce Semiconductors.
Enacted as H.R.3684 (117th). Marketed as the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law. 'IIJA'/'BIL' are display-only acronyms.
Enacted as H.R.1319 (117th). 'ARPA' collides with the Advanced Research Projects Agency, so it is display-only.

names: Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act
phrases: coronavirus aid, relief, and economic security
aliases.note: Enacted as H.R.748 (116th). Predates the corpus window but its implementation is still lobbied/discussed 2022+.
Enacted as S.2938 (117th). The post-Uvalde gun-safety law.
Enacted as H.R.3746 (118th). The 2023 debt-limit agreement. 'debt ceiling deal' is a display name only (too generic to match).

names: Sergeant First Class Heath Robinson Honoring our Promise to Address Comprehensive Toxics Act
aliases.note: Enacted as S.3373 (117th), the veterans toxic-exposure (burn pits) law. Phrase deliberately restricted to 'honoring our pact act': bare 'PACT Act' also names the older Preventing Animal Cruelty and Torture Act (2019), so it is display-only.

Enacted as Division T of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2023 (H.R.2617, 117th, P.L. 117-328). The House standalone vehicle was H.R.2954 (Securing a Strong Retirement Act of 2022). Because H.R.2617 is a broad omnibus, a number match on HR2617 is NOT specific to SECURE 2.0 retirement provisions; treat HR2617 hits as omnibus lobbying and lean on the name phrases. Medium confidence on completeness of vehicles.

Recurring annual vehicle -> a different number every fiscal year, so this entry lists one enacted number per recent FY and is phrase-first for cross-year work. FY2022 = S.1605 (117th, P.L. 117-81); FY2023 = H.R.7776 (117th, P.L. 117-263); FY2024 = H.R.2670 (118th, P.L. 118-31); FY2025 = H.R.5009 (118th, P.L. 118-159). A number match here mixes fiscal years; use the year facets in lda_bill_lookup.py output to separate them.

PHRASE-PRIMARY BY DESIGN, no 'bills' listed. The standing law is the Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018 = H.R.2 of the 115th Congress, but 'H.R.2' is reassigned to an unrelated bill every Congress (H.R.2 in the 117th/118th/119th are NOT the Farm Bill), so listing HR2 as a match number would fabricate hits. This is the canonical L004 lesson: the Farm Bill is reliably found by NAME, never by number. lda_bill_lookup.py name-matches these phrases against press text AND filing free-text (senate activity descriptions, house specific_issues).
